item.description:
ChainLink 0.3.482

The quality-of-life release — a big one.

  • New manual chapter: "Which rider are you?" — 17 short rider profiles pointing you at the parts of the app built for you. Start there if the manual feels long.
  • Tire pressure calculator on every bike page (the published Rene Herse approach): weight, luggage, tire width, casing, surface — front and rear pressures in psi and bar. Describe your wheels and the AI fills in published rim/tire widths. Tires can also split into front + rear for separate wear tracking (rears wear faster) — and rejoin any time.
  • AI Coach settings, redesigned — one pick-one list of engines, privacy options on top, a model picker per cloud engine.
  • Events — FKT attempts with target times; multi-day events with daily targets that shape the plan; brevet records driving Audax/SR progress; Training → Import a plan brings a coach's plan in intact.
  • Aspirations — write a goal in your own words on the Goals page; the coach tracks it against your real numbers, no empty cheer.
  • Session fuelling guidance — every session's detail shows what to eat, scaled to that session: recovery spins say "water is enough", long hard days get 90–120 g/hr with a glucose+fructose note, and there's a practical per-hour food list with a whole-session total.
  • Personal heatmap — opens on your home riding area; click any line to open that ride (several rides on one road offer a short list).
  • Weather day call — on an unrideable day, the Overview weather card suggests what to do instead: your cross-training sports, the trainer, or an earned day off, backed by your training data.
  • Navigation fixes — sidebar clicks now always land, even from a pushed page (heatmap, ride, bike detail); clicking outside a pop-up card closes it; a "Setting things up" banner could get stuck — fixed.
  • Week start — Monday or Sunday weeks (Settings → Preferences); every weekly stat follows.
  • Plus: bikes can be marked out of commission / away / borrowed; Settings had a design pass; an invite-a-tester QR code lives in Settings → About; faster page loads and assorted fixes.

ChainLink 0.3.455

A big update — everything since 0.3.438, plus a fix.

  • Fixed: the "Setting things up…" banner could get stuck on screen after launch even though your data was fully loaded. It now always clears.
  • New manual chapter: "Which rider are you?" — 17 short rider profiles (racer, randonneur, tourer, commuter, indoor rider, returning from a break…) that point you at the parts of the app built for you. Start there if the manual feels long.
  • AI Coach settings, redesigned — one clear pick-one list of engines, privacy options on top, and a model picker for each cloud engine (use exactly the model you want).
  • Settings page design pass — simpler layout, less clutter, details tucked into expanding sections; Import and Preferences are now separate cards.
  • Events — FKT attempts with target times and an attempts log; multi-day events with daily distance/time targets that shape your training plan; brevet records with Audax/Super Randonneur progress built from your real rides.
  • Bring your coach's plan — Training → Import a plan: paste it, review what was read, adopt it. ChainLink tracks compliance without rewriting your coach's structure.
  • Bikes — mark a bike out of commission, away in another location, or borrowed/rented; ride assignment and totals respect it.
  • Aspirations — write a goal in your own words on the Goals page ("I want to get faster at club crits") and the coach tracks it against your actual numbers.
  • Week start — choose Monday or Sunday weeks (Settings → Preferences); all weekly stats follow.
  • Weather day call — on a genuinely unrideable day the Overview weather card now suggests what to do instead: your cross-training sports, the trainer, or an earned day off, backed by your training data.
  • Plus: Rides refresh automatically after a Sync, faster page loads, and assorted fixes.

ChainLink 0.3.454

A big update — everything since 0.3.438.

  • New manual chapter: "Which rider are you?" — 17 short rider profiles (racer, randonneur, tourer, commuter, indoor rider, returning from a break…) that point you at the parts of the app built for you. Start there if the manual feels long.
  • AI Coach settings, redesigned — one clear pick-one list of engines, privacy options on top, and a model picker for each cloud engine (use exactly the model you want).
  • Settings page design pass — simpler layout, less clutter, details tucked into expanding sections; Import and Preferences are now separate cards.
  • Events — FKT attempts with target times and an attempts log; multi-day events with daily distance/time targets that shape your training plan; brevet records with Audax/Super Randonneur progress built from your real rides.
  • Bring your coach's plan — Training → Import a plan: paste it, review what was read, adopt it. ChainLink tracks compliance without rewriting your coach's structure.
  • Bikes — mark a bike out of commission, away in another location, or borrowed/rented; ride assignment and totals respect it.
  • Aspirations — write a goal in your own words on the Goals page ("I want to get faster at club crits") and the coach tracks it against your actual numbers.
  • Week start — choose Monday or Sunday weeks (Settings → Preferences); all weekly stats follow.
  • Weather day call — on a genuinely unrideable day the Overview weather card now suggests what to do instead: your cross-training sports, the trainer, or an earned day off, backed by your training data.
  • Plus: Rides refresh automatically after a Sync, faster page loads, and assorted fixes.
